summary: Rain‑soaked 6/28/85 delivers a legendary performance (Terrapin, Birdsong, Tom Thumb) captured on a muddy but listenable audience recording.
review: The show is remembered as one of the high points of the 1985 summer tour – a rain‑battered night that sparked unforgettable versions of Terrapin Station, Estimated Prophet, Bird Song and Tom Thumb Blues.  While the source is an audience/FOB tape with raindrop‑muffled highs and uneven fidelity, most listeners find it surprisingly clear for a crowd recording and worth the experience.

ai_rating.rationale: The overwhelming majority of reviews praise the musical performance (many 5‑star) while noting only moderate technical flaws; the weighted average yields a strong rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Muddy, rain‑affected highs and occasional low‑end boominess typical of an FOB/audience tape, but generally clear enough to enjoy; several reviewers call it above‑average for a crowd recording.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Fans describe a strong first set and an even more energetic second set; the rain adds atmosphere but never detracts from the tight, high‑energy performance that defines the summer ’85 tour.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for soaring Bird Song jam and his charismatic stage presence (velvety paisley jacket); a few note vocal strain typical of ’85.
band_member_comments.Keys: Positive nods to the Brent‑era keyboards, described as solid throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Drumming highlighted as groovy and extra‑tight, especially on Tom Thumb Blues and the space segment.
